# WI-QUIC-CRT-0019 - QUIC CRT client-role 1-RTT readiness work item

## Summary

Add the client-role post-Finished 1-RTT readiness seam so the supported active-loopback data-path can open after Finished proof without widening the supported surface.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0121

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0019

## Planned Changes

- Update `QuicTlsKeySchedule.ProcessFinished(...)` so the client role publishes `KeysAvailable` for `OneRtt` plus directional 1-RTT packet-protection material after peer Finished verification.
- Tighten `QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.TryMarkOneRttKeysAvailable()` so it rejects premature `OneRtt` key publication before `PeerFinishedVerified`.
- Add or update CRT requirement-home coverage for the client Finished 1-RTT readiness boundary and the premature-publication rejection path.
- Add a permanent benchmark for the client Finished publication path.
- Keep retry unsupported; do not widen the public boundary or `IsSupported`.

## Out of Scope

- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- public API widening
- trust-store policy
- hostname validation
- certificate-path validation
- revocation
- 0-RTT
- key update
- broader 1-RTT data-path ownership
- interop harness dispatch

## Verification Plan

Run the new CRT requirement-home tests, the affected API loopback guards, and the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep. Run the permanent benchmark suite for the client Finished publication path and validate the spec-trace artifacts after rendering.

## Completion Notes

Optional implementation notes, deviations, or follow-up items.
